exercises.title: Trip Time Calculator
exercises.description: Calculate road trip details from the given variables. Print travel time in hours and minutes, fuel needed in liters, and fuel cost.
exercises.starterCode:
# Trip data (pre-set variables)
distance_km = 360.0
avg_speed_kmh = 90.0
fuel_efficiency_km_per_liter = 12.0
fuel_price_per_liter = 1.50

# TODO: Calculate total_hours = distance_km / avg_speed_kmh
# TODO: Calculate full_hours = int(total_hours)
# TODO: Calculate remaining_minutes = int((total_hours - full_hours) * 60)
# TODO: Calculate liters_needed = distance_km / fuel_efficiency_km_per_liter
# TODO: Calculate total_fuel_cost = liters_needed * fuel_price_per_liter

# TODO: Print 'Travel time: 4 hours 0 minutes'
# TODO: Print 'Fuel needed: 30.0 liters'
# TODO: Print 'Fuel cost: $45.00'
exercises.expectedOutput:
Travel time: 4 hours 0 minutes
Fuel needed: 30.0 liters
Fuel cost: $45.00
hints: Use int() to convert the float hours to a whole number.
The fractional hours * 60 gives remaining minutes.
Use f-string formatting: f"${total_fuel_cost:.2f}" for 2 decimal places.

exercises.title: Simple Report Card
exercises.description: Generate a formatted report card from the given student data. Calculate the average and display a complete summary.
exercises.starterCode:
# Student data
student_name = "Maria"
math_grade = 88.0
english_grade = 92.0
science_grade = 79.0

# TODO: Calculate average (sum of 3 grades / 3)

# TODO: Print '=== Report Card ==='
# TODO: Print 'Student: Maria'
# TODO: Print 'Math:    88.0'
# TODO: Print 'English: 92.0'
# TODO: Print 'Science: 79.0'
# TODO: Print 'Average: 86.3'
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Report Card ===
Student: Maria
Math:    88.0
English: 92.0
Science: 79.0
Average: 86.3
hints: Use f"{average:.1f}" to display the average with 1 decimal place.

exercises.title: Subscription Cost Summary
exercises.description: Calculate the total monthly and annual cost for three subscriptions, then print a formatted summary with all costs.
exercises.starterCode:
# Subscription costs (monthly)
streaming = 14.99
music = 9.99
cloud_storage = 2.99

# TODO: Calculate monthly_total
# TODO: Calculate annual_total = monthly_total * 12

# TODO: Print the following formatted output:
# --- Subscriptions ---
# Streaming:     $14.99/mo
# Music:          $9.99/mo
# Cloud storage:  $2.99/mo
# Monthly total: $27.97
# Annual total: $335.64
exercises.expectedOutput:
--- Subscriptions ---
Streaming:     $14.99/mo
Music:          $9.99/mo
Cloud storage:  $2.99/mo
Monthly total: $27.97
Annual total: $335.64
hints: Use f"{streaming:.2f}" to format to 2 decimal places.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: 4-Step Development Process
warmup.description: A complete program has: input (or pre-set data), calculations, and formatted output. Trace through this simple program.
warmup.starterCode:
# Step 1: Data
length = 5
width = 3

# Step 2: Calculate
area = length * width
perimeter = 2 * (length + width)

# Step 3: Output
print(f"Area: {area}")
print(f"Perimeter: {perimeter}")
